title: Step 1
text: When the healthcare facility receives a report from an external party about the occurrence of a disaster, the security team must first verify the source of the report, the type and magnitude of the disaster, the disaster location, and the approximate number and type of expected casualties as available through the code announcement.

title: Step 2
text: Announce Code Yellow through the internal overhead speaker system and send a signal through wireless communication devices to the security teams assigned for emergency response.

title: Step 3
text: Activate the facility external disaster emergency plan and implement the relevant internal preparedness procedures.

title: Step 4
text: Coordinate with ambulance command, emergency departments, and the planning and emergency preparedness unit to raise the level of readiness and preparedness for security personnel in the receiving area.

title: Step 5
text: Prepare ambulance access points and control movement routes by assigning one-way entry paths to emergency and ambulance sections, while facilitating arrival to the triage area.

title: Step 6
text: At the main external gate, direct minor cases and low-severity patients under medical supervision to other nearby hospitals according to the external emergency plan.

title: Step 7
text: Adhere strictly to access control policies related to entry into ambulance and emergency departments, and permit access only to authorized persons.

title: Step 8
text: Ensure full CCTV coverage of the ambulance and emergency zones, including triage and patient transport areas, and maintain active monitoring during the event.

title: Step 9
text: Prevent crowd gathering or photography around the triage area and ambulance/emergency perimeter.

title: Step 10
text: Direct families of casualties and injured persons to the designated authority in the facility for information and coordination.

title: Step 11
text: Apply strict procedures for receiving, transferring, and handling deceased persons in coordination with authorized specialists and the facility mortuary.

title: Step 12
text: Ensure that all records and belongings related to casualties, injured persons, and deceased persons are preserved according to facility policies and procedures.

title: Step 13
text: When a pre-activated response plan exists for biological, chemical, or radiological weapons, security personnel must implement required personal protective equipment measures and comply with instructions issued by the Hazmat Response Team.

title: Step 14
text: When a pre-activated response plan exists for epidemic or infectious disease events, security personnel must implement required personal protective equipment measures and comply with precautionary procedures according to the relevant public health guidance, in coordination with the Infection Control Department.

title: Step 15
text: Communicate with the relevant authority to provide the necessary personal protective equipment for security staff and ensure that security personnel have received the required vaccinations when applicable.

title: Step 16
text: Code Red may be announced during activation of the external disaster emergency plan in situations including, but not limited to, when the number of casualties exceeds the facility response capacity or when an internal disaster such as a fire occurs.

title: Step 17
text: After the end of an internal or external disaster, continue security presence to secure the area, facilitate the entry and exit of ambulances, and support return to normal operations only after receiving the approved security directions.

title: Step 18
text: After the event, evaluate the performance of security staff and the security plan, identify weaknesses and gaps, extract lessons learned, circulate them to security personnel, and submit recommendations to facility leadership.
